title: camellia japonica subsp. hortensis
intro: Camellia japonica subsp. hortensis, commonly known as the camellia, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the tea family (Theaceae) and is native to Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. The health benefits of Camellia japonica subsp. hortensis include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of certain types of cancer, and enhanced cognitive function. Camellia japonica subsp. hortensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs and plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
